etymology.description: Depicts an elephant: ⺈ represents the trunk, 𫩏 represents the head, and 𧰨 represents the body
etymology.interpretation: The character 象(xiàng, "elephant") is a direct pictorial representation of an elephant, emphasizing its prominent features such as the trunk, head, and body to convey its meaning.

examples.chinese: 大象是陆地上最大的动物(大象是陸地上最大的動物)。
examples.pinyin: Dàxiàng shì lùdì shàng zuì dà de dòngwù.
examples.english: The elephant is the largest animal on land.

examples.chinese: 在非洲的草原上(在非洲的草原上)，你能看到许多野生的大象(你能看到許多野生的大象)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Fēizhōu de cǎoyuán shàng，nǐ néng kàn dào xǔduō yěshēng de dàxiàng.
examples.english: On the African savannah, you can see many wild elephants.

examples.chinese: 我第一次看到大象是在动物园(我第一次看到大象是在動物園)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ dì yī cì kàn dào dàxiàng shì zài dòngwùyuán.
examples.english: The first time I saw an elephant was at the zoo.
